title: Web Components in Large Organizations
content: Web Components are a great fit when multiple teams use different frameworks but need one UI language. They enforce a clean boundary with native browser primitives.
The hardest part is not component code. It is documentation quality, migration support, and making examples realistic enough for product teams.
At scale, backward compatibility matters more than shipping many new components. A small stable set creates trust and lowers integration costs.
Use design tokens and strict component APIs. That combination lets teams customize themes without rewriting behavior.
